value.Title: April 28, 2026: Orrick Adds Experienced USPTO Veteran to Expand IP Practice

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JHR-F9P3-SCRX-631K-00000-00April 28, 2026: Orrick Adds Experienced USPTO Veteran to Expand IP Practice2026-05-07T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:37:39ZLexisNexisANNOUNCEMENTSApril 28, 2026: Orrick Adds Experienced USPTO Veteran to Expand IP Practice

Orrick, Herrington & Sutcliffe has expanded its IP practice by hiring a senior attorney from the USPTO, a move that reflects the firm's ongoing push into technology and telecommunications sectors.

The new attorney will officially join on the 28th of April, 2026, bringing a wealth of experience from the USPTO, including patent examination and litigation.

The hire is expected to augment Orrick's ability to guide clients through patent strategy and protection in rapidly changing markets.

Orrick's managing partner commented that the addition reinforces the firm's commitment to best-in-class IP services, with professionals who understand patent law's intricacies.

Industry observers note that the firm's ability to attract talent from leading institutions could influence its growth trajectory and competitive position in technology-driven sectors.

INDEX

SECTION 1 ORRICK, HERRINGTON & SUTCLIFFE LLP PROFILE

SECTION 1 ORRICK, HERRINGTON & SUTCLIFFE LLP PROFILE

1.1 ACTIVITIES

Orrick, Herrington & Sutcliffe LLP, founded in 1863, is a global law firm headquartered in San Francisco, California. With over 1,100 lawyers across 25 offices, it serves technology, life sciences, and energy clients, specializing in M&A, IP, and venture capital. In 2024, it advised Coatue Management on $11.35 billion in investments, earning recognition as a top firm for startups and SaaS companies. Its culture fosters collaboration, diversity, and innovation, with key lawyers like Mark Solakian driving tech deals. Competing with Cooley, Orrick stands out for its tech-sector expertise and client-side advice. Recent initiatives include expanding AI and cybersecurity practices.

Mission

Orrick, Herrington & Sutcliffe LLP (Orrick) stands as a global leader in the legal industry, renowned for innovation, collaboration, and inclusivity. With over 25 worldwide offices and expertise spanning Technology & Innovation, Energy & Infrastructure, Finance, and Life Sciences & HealthTech, Orrick provides forward-thinking legal services tailored to client needs. Recognized for excellence in venture capital, renewable energy transactions, and high-stakes litigation, the firm prides itself on its commitment to diversity and cutting-edge solutions.

Vision

Orrick envisions establishing itself as a trusted global partner, offering innovative legal solutions that empower clients to grow, innovate, and succeed in rapidly evolving industries. Their goal is to redefine legal services by blending expertise with creativity to deliver comprehensive advice.

Values

Orrick's foundation is built upon the following core values:

Excellence: Upholding the highest standards of professionalism and quality in every service.

Teamwork: Encouraging collaboration within and beyond the firm to achieve outstanding outcomes.

Innovation: Committed to redefining legal practices through creative, client-centric approaches.

Perseverance: Tenaciously working to deliver the best possible outcomes for clients.

Inclusivity: Fostering a diverse and equitable environment where everyone is empowered to thrive.

By combining these principles, Orrick continues to elevate its reputation as a frontrunner in the global legal landscape.

1.2 SUMMARY

value.Title: Di Pilla plans to make life simpler for HMC Capital

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JHH-WK33-RX99-J0DW-00000-00Di Pilla plans to make life simpler for HMC Capital2026-05-07T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:37:39ZLexisNexisBUSINESSPg. 57Di Pilla plans to make life simpler for HMC CapitalBen Wilmot

Investment banker turned fund manager David Di Pilla has shaken up his beaten-down HMC Capital empire, with its listed digital trust selling a $1bn data centre in Chicago, warning fewer of the US complexes will be built than expected.

Mr Di Pilla said only two of every 10 data centres proposed in the US would actually be built and he is instead pouring capital back into Australia.

Mr Di Pilla predicted Australia would offer more sustainable energy supply and pointed to bans on data centre projects in 14 US states.

The company's DigiCo Infrastructure REIT also hit hurdles in Los Angeles and plans to get out of that market.

Mr Di Pilla is instead backing the expansion of his DigiCo trust's Sydney data centre, which is winning customers much faster than expected.

The pullback from the US operation follows investors losing faith in the offshore expansion strategy and DigiCo's share price collapsing.

The sale is part of a bigger shift to simplify the sprawling HMC Capital empire, which has stumbled not only in data centres but also in healthcare real estate, where it is a Healthscope landlord and private equity investing.

On that front, the company effectively terminated the fund it used to drive the Chemist Warehouse-Sigma deal, after the trust copped heavy losses this year. Mr Di Pilla cited its early successes but noted the clash between running a corporate activist strategy in the listed market and the demands of gatekeepers like asset consultants, which wanted it to invest widely.

The fund has instead been stuck in slumping stocks like Lendlease, Baby Bunting and Ingenia Communities. It sold out of most of its stake in Lifestyle Communities, leaving it with about $100m in cash.

Mr Di Pilla touted HMC Capital's ability to combine renewable energy, where it took a large bet after buying a $950m portfolio of wind, storage and solar projects from Brookfield, and data centres. That energy bet weighed on the company's balance sheet, but the fund manager insists it will pay off, despite not revealing the company's long-term plans for the assets. Mr Di Pilla is trying to win back the faith of the sharemarket after his investors all but abandoned HMC Capital because its ambitions of becoming a $50bn empire were not in keeping with tougher times. But, he has pointed to cost cutting and the firm's ability to pivot in a world that he said was about to get tougher.

value.Title: SparkzCore22 Announcement: From factory floor to power grid - Sparkz launches first U.S.-built battery system

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JKH-1BD3-RW07-P2GN-00000-00SparkzCore22 Announcement: From factory floor to power grid - Sparkz launches first U.S.-built battery system2026-05-06T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:37:39ZLexisNexisSparkzCore22 Announcement: From factory floor to power grid - Sparkz launches first U.S.-built battery system

Key Highlights:

* Sparkz launches first U.S.-built battery system for homes, businesses, and Virtual Power Plants.

* New battery system uses cobalt-free, US-Made Lithium Iron Phosphate batteries for safer, longer-lasting energy storage.

* SparkzCore22 supports grid stability by balancing supply and demand, advancing U.S. manufacturing with domestically produced battery technology.

Original Press Release:

Sacramento, California, May 6 -- SPARKZ, Inc.issued the following news release:

From factory floor to power grid: Sparkz launches first U.S.-built battery system

Sparkz unveiled its new U.S.-manufactured battery system today that will help power homes, businesses, schools while taking the burden off the strained power grid.

The global move toward sustainable energy is no longer about technology, it's a race to scale manufacturing and secure supply chains. Sparkz is pulling ahead by delivering solutions with SparkzCore22. The new battery system is enabling AI data centers to store 25% more power into the same footprint.

To kick off Sacramento Climate Week, Sparkz debuted the SparkzCore22 at the MAP (Metro Air Parkway) facility, with a media tour Wednesday.

SparkzCore22 follows a "BYOB model: Bring Your Own Batteries", allowing customers to plug in scalable battery storage that can power homes, support businesses, or connect into a Virtual Power Plant to strengthen the grid.

CEO and Founder, Sanjiv Malhotra and the Sparkz team demonstrated the SparkzCore22 using US-Made Lithium Iron Phosphate batteries, which deliver a safer, longer-lasting alternative than traditional batteries. The system stores energy in advanced batteries and delivers it on demand, bringing solutions to Virtual Power Plants (VPP) for supporting Data centers and AI infrastructure across the country.

"Energy demand is growing rapidly, and the grid needs smarter, more resilient solutions, said Malhotra. "We are the only LFP battery manufacturer in the U.S. establishing those solutions with the SparkzCore22."

The cobalt-free battery system is designed to:

* Power homes and businesses with reliable, scalable energy storage

*Enable Virtual Power Plants (VPP)

*Support grid stability by balancing supply and demand

*Advance U.S. manufacturing with domestically produced battery technology

This is the Sparkz way.

The only company succeeding in establishing the LFP battery ecosystem in the US. We are making this from scratch-high-performance material made from abundant American materials, such as lithium, iron, phosphate, Cells and the complete Battery system.

[Category: Renewable Energy, Energy, New Offerings, Supply Chain and Procurement, Technology, ESG and Sustainability, Innovation]

value.Title: Researchers at China University of Petroleum Target Nanoneedles (Rational construction of unique cobalt hydroxide fluoride microflower decorated with platinum-tin alloy onto nickel foam enabling efficient overall water splitting at ampere-level ...)

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JH0-WGW3-S8R1-11XJ-00000-00Researchers at China University of Petroleum Target Nanoneedles (Rational construction of unique cobalt hydroxide fluoride microflower decorated with platinum-tin alloy onto nickel foam enabling efficient overall water splitting at ampere-level ...)2026-05-04T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:37:39ZLexisNexisNANOTECHNOLOGY - NANONEEDLESResearchers at China University of Petroleum Target Nanoneedles (Rational construction of unique cobalt hydroxide fluoride microflower decorated with platinum-tin alloy onto nickel foam enabling efficient overall water splitting at ampere-level ...)


2026 MAY 04 (NewsRx) -- By a News Reporter-Staff News Editor at Nanotech Daily -- Current study results on Nanotechnology - Nanoneedles have been published. According to news reporting out of Beijing, People's Republic of China, by NewsRx editors, research stated, "The development of efficient electrocatalysts capable of stable operation at ampere-level current densities is essential for industrial water electrolysis. Herein, based on the triple synergistic strategy of 'hydrogen spillover-interfacial microenvironment regulation-wettability management', platinum-tin (PtSn) nano-alloy modified with dahlia-like cobalt hydroxide fluoride (Co(OH)F) grown on nickel foam (NF), denoted as PtSn/Co(OH)F/NF, was fabricated via hydrothermal and electrochemical deposition techniques."


Our news journalists obtained a quote from the research from the China University of Petroleum, "Toward hydrogen evolution reaction (HER), the hydrogen spillover effect is driven by a Gibbs free energy of hydrogen adsorption (DG) gradient between PtSn alloy and Co(OH)F carrier, leading to a significant increase in the active surface area at low platinum loadings. Additionally, tin (Sn) alloying accelerates water splitting kinetics by modulating the d-band center of platinum (Pt). For the oxygen evolution reaction (OER), the nanoneedle-induced tip effect enhances local electric fields, promoting hydroxide ion (OH) enrichment and transport within the electrical double layer. Furthermore, the hierarchical 'nanoneedle-nanoribbon-microflower' structure creates superhydrophilic and gas-repellent surface, facilitating rapid bubble release and mass transfer under high current densities. Thus, PtSn/Co(OH)F/NF delivered an overpotential of 15 ± 2 mV at 10 mA cm, and merely 212 ± 1 mV and 378 ± 2 mV to achieve 1 A cm for HER and OER, respectively. Notably, cell voltages of only 1.47 and 1.68 V are required to achieve 10 and 500 mA cm, with an operational stability of 240 h. The hydrogen spillover mechanism was confirmed via density functional theory (DFT) calculation."
According to the news editors, the research concluded: "This rational design strategy yields low-Pt electrocatalysts with optimized morphology and high intrinsic activity, offering a promising route for green energy applications."
This research has been peer-reviewed.
For more information on this research see: Rational construction of unique cobalt hydroxide fluoride microflower decorated with platinum-tin alloy onto nickel foam enabling efficient overall water splitting at ampere-level current densities. Journal of Colloid and Interface Science, 2026;718:140556. Journal of Colloid and Interface Science can be contacted at: Academic Press Inc Elsevier Science, 525 B St, Ste 1900, San Diego, CA 92101-4495, USA. (Elsevier - www.elsevier.com; Journal of Colloid and Interface Science - www.journals.elsevier.com/journal-of-colloid-and-interface-science/)
Our news journalists report that additional information may be obtained by contacting Meitong Zhao, State Key Laboratory of Heavy Oil Processing, China University of Petroleum, Beijing 102249, People's Republic of China. Additional authors for this research include Fan Yang, Xinyang Sun, Yang Sun, Junpu An, Hongchen Liu, Jiahui Liu, Conghan Zhang and Yongfeng Li.
Publisher contact information for the Journal of Colloid and Interface Science is: Academic Press Inc Elsevier Science, 525 B St, Ste 1900, San Diego, CA 92101-4495, USA.
Keywords for this news article include: Beijing, People's Republic of China, Asia, Alkalies, Anions, Cobalt, Elements, Emerging Technologies, Fluorides, Gases, Hydrofluoric Acid, Hydrogen, Hydroxides, Inorganic Chemicals, Nanoneedles, Nanotechnology, Nickel, Transition Elements.

value.Title: Clean Power Alliance Expanded Access to Affordable Clean Energy for Disadvantaged Communities Across Southern California

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JH5-Y113-S5Y1-6064-00000-00Clean Power Alliance Expanded Access to Affordable Clean Energy for Disadvantaged Communities Across Southern California2026-05-02T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:37:39ZLexisNexisClean Power Alliance Expanded Access to Affordable Clean Energy for Disadvantaged Communities Across Southern California

Los Angeles: Civic leaders, elected officials and community stakeholders gathered in the city of Carson today to celebrate a significant milestone in SouthernCalifornia ’ s clean energy transition: the launch of a new rooftop community solar installation that expands access to renewable energy and lowers energy bills for income-qualified households through Clean Power Alliance ’ s (CPA) Power Share program.

The new installation is among 16 solar photovoltaic projects that CPA has in operation or under development, totaling approximately 23 megawatts of renewable energy capacity. Industrial real estate company Prologis is the developer for 10 of these projects, representing 9 megawatts of capacity and creating more than 160 jobs by utilizing roof space on large warehouse facilities.

These savings provide critical relief for families disproportionately impacted by high energy bills,said CPA CEO Ted Bardacke. We are proud to help expand equitable access to renewable energy solutions that strengthen communities, lower costs and ensure Southern California ’ s clean energy future benefits everyone.

The celebratory event, hosted by CPA and Prologis, marked the activation of the Carson South Bay 20 project, a 1.32-megawatt rooftop solar installation that will provide 100% renewable energy at reduced rates to approximately 500 local households. Discounted renewable energy will also be provided to City of Carson, a CPA member, which will save approximately $75,000 per year on energy costs at their city hall.

This project is a powerful example of how collaboration between public agencies, private industry and community stakeholders can deliver meaningful results,said City of Carson Mayor Lula Davis-Holmes. This program makes clean energy savings accessible to renters and working families who have too often been excluded from the benefits of the clean energy economy.

value.Title: NUVVE JAPAN Appoints Shigeki Mori, Former THECOO CFO, as Executive Officer of Finance
value.Overview: MarketLine NewsWire (Formerly Datamonitor), TELECOMS; Technology & Telecommunications, 377words

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JH0-S623-S8HS-S28K-00000-00NUVVE JAPAN Appoints Shigeki Mori, Former THECOO CFO, as Executive Officer of Finance2026-05-01T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:37:39ZLexisNexisTELECOMSTechnology & TelecommunicationsNUVVE JAPAN Appoints Shigeki Mori, Former THECOO CFO, as Executive Officer of Finance

NUVVE JAPAN K.K. announced the appointment of Shigeki Mori as Executive Officer of Finance (CFO), effective May 2026.

NUVVE JAPAN K.K. ("NVJ"), a leading provider of energy management and Vehicle-to-Grid (V2G) solutions and a subsidiary of U.S.-based Nuvve Holding Corp., today announced it has appointed Shigeki Mori as Executive Officer of Finance (CFO), effective May 2026. Mori is a specialist in startup finance with a proven track record of leading high-growth technology companies to the public market. He most recently served as Director and CFO of THECOO Inc., where he successfully spearheaded the company's IPO on the Tokyo Stock Exchange Mothers (now Growth) Market. "NUVVE JAPAN is at the forefront of the energy storage revolution," said Mori.

"We are focused on transforming the company's cutting-edge control technology into a robust portfolio of yield-generating assets. My mission is to build a financial foundation that accelerates our capital investments, achieves an early IPO, and delivers sustainable value to our stakeholders as we build the energy infrastructure of the future." NVJ is a Resource Aggregator and integrates grid-scale storage batteries and V2G chargers into a Virtual Power Plant (VPP) platform. NVJ now enters a new phase of growth amid the urgent global need for grid stability and renewable energy optimization. The company plans to evolve into an "Asset-Holding Model," owning and operating its own storage batteries and V2G infrastructure to maximize long-term revenue. NVJ will focus on the following strategic pillars: Establish large-scale investment schemes for grid-scale batteries and V2G infrastructure. Maximize Lifetime Value (LTV) through the ownership of battery assets and implement agile capital management responsive to energy market fluctuations. Strengthen corporate governance and engage in strategic investor relations to drive enterprise value in preparation for an early listing. Mori graduated from Keio University's Faculty of Commerce and later earned his MBA from the University of Southern California (USC). He held senior management and finance positions at several multinational corporations before joining the startup ecosystem to lead financial department restructuring and fundraising efforts. He was appointed Director and CFO of THECOO Inc. in 2018 and led the company to a successful IPO in December 2021.

value.Title: He's building a business in Buffalo after a decade away

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JK5-3F93-RVK1-3021-00000-00He's building a business in Buffalo after a decade away2026-05-14T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:37:39ZLexisNexisHe's building a business in Buffalo after a decade awayLian Bunny

Traveling abroad sparked Jared Perram's passion for renewable energy, but taking charge of his own career and happiness led him to plant roots back home in Buffalo.

Over 16 years ago, Perram, an Orchard Park native, studied for a semester through the University of Virginia's Semester at Sea program. A converted cruise liner became the students' floating classroom for three months as they visited about nine different countries.

It was his first exposure to the renewable energy investments in many Scandinavian countries. He wanted to pair that with his business degree from Buffalo State University.

"It just felt very obvious to me and it felt like a path or an industry that was emerging that I could certainly get in on the bottom floor," he said. "I see logic in the renewables and I see logic in how I can pair all of this up."

Why it matters: Buffalo benefits from locals moving away and returning home, as they bring new perspectives, experiences and an appreciation for WesternNew York with them. Entrepreneurs like Perram are adding to our small business ecosystem and economy.

After moving away from the region for a decade to work in renewable energy in Charlotte and California, he moved to Rochester to be closer to home and due to New York's need for professionals like him in solar programs.

He left that role after a few years, stepped away from renewables entirely, moved to Buffalo and started planning his next move. In 2024, he began a consulting business that didn't take off and had trouble finding work he was excited about.

"One morning I woke up... and just decided, you know what, I'm done putting any of my future, my happiness or my wellbeing in anyone else's hands," he said. "I'm just going all in on a cleaning business."

He began Transparent Property Services, a cleaning and related services business, in July 2025. He's still a one-man operation but has grown the mobile business from around three jobs a week to two jobs per day booked out through mid-June.

As his full-time job, he focuses on customer service, from good communication to showing up on time to fixing mistakes if they happen. He uses the combination of his experiences - working with contractors through renewable energy and project management roles and landscaping and construction jobs when he was younger.

Perram likes that his work and company are in his own hands and are in an "AI-proof" industry, while utilizing AI integrations for back-end operations to boost efficiency.

He's also focused on search-engine optimization, leading him to make moves that aren't requirements for his business like getting Better Business Bureau accredited and obtaining his contractor's license through the City of Buffalo.

His main clients are individual homeowners, and he's found that people here care and want to invest in how their home looks, regardless of whether they're middle-class or luxury-residence owners. Folks also value hard work.

"I think that this type of market, Buffalo, is an interesting market for businesses like this, for sort of blue collar, independent businesses," Perram said. "It is a community that embraces hard work. I don't know that I could start this company in California."

Since returning to Buffalo, he's also had a deeper appreciation for many of the region's benefits, from proximity to family to the bigger city feel within a smaller city. The cost of living is preferable to California's, he said, and he can be in the forest or country with his canoe via a 15-minute drive.

"I think I appreciate much more now this kind of little corner of the world that we have," he said. "I love what the city's doing and I want to be a part of it."

Do you know a regional business professional who exemplifies what Buffalo could use more of, from a local returning home to a business owner with no local ties building a company here? Email Lbunny@bizjournals.com with your suggestion.

Did you find this article useful? Why not subscribe to Buffalo Business First for more articles?

value.Title: April 24, 2026: Dynamic Cables Partners with TS Conductor to Modernize India's Grid with HTLS Solutions

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JJV-SKF3-SGFR-X37G-00000-00April 24, 2026: Dynamic Cables Partners with TS Conductor to Modernize India's Grid with HTLS Solutions2026-05-13T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:37:39ZLexisNexisANNOUNCEMENTSApril 24, 2026: Dynamic Cables Partners with TS Conductor to Modernize India's Grid with HTLS Solutions

Under the agreement, Dynamic Cables will leverage TS Conductor's patented Aluminum Encapsulated Carbon Core technology to produce HTLS carbon core conductors. These products are designed to increase grid capacity and reduce transmission losses by up to 50%, offering a route to upgrade performance without new infrastructure. The collaboration is set to run from 24th April 2026 for an initial 12-month period and includes a license to use TS designs at Dynamic Cables’ Indian facilities, with the option to renew.

INDEX

SECTION 1 TS CONDUCTOR PROFILE

SECTION 1 TS CONDUCTOR PROFILE

1.1 ACTIVITIES

TS Conductor is a U.S.-based energy technology company headquartered in Huntington Beach, California, founded in 2018. As a public benefit corporation, it specializes in manufacturing next-generation advanced conductors, particularly its patented Aluminum Encapsulated Carbon Core (AECC) technology. This innovative solution enables utilities to double or triple transmission capacity, reduce line losses, improve reliability, and lower overall project costs for grid upgrades and new lines, while remaining fully compatible with traditional ACSR/ACSS installation and maintenance practices. The company serves the electric utility sector, supporting the integration of renewable energy sources, grid modernization, and rising electricity demand from data centers and electrification trends. With thousands of miles installed since 2016 and backing from leading investors, TS Conductor is experiencing rapid expansion, highlighted by significant manufacturing investments, including a new facility in South Carolina. The fastest-growing segments include reconductoring projects for existing transmission lines and solutions addressing surging demand in high-growth areas like AI-driven data centers and renewable integration.

1.2 SUMMARY

Website: https://tsconductor.com/

Dynamic Cables has entered into a strategic technology alliance with TS Conductor Corp, enabling the Indian company to manufacture HTLS carbon core conductors from 24th April 2026. The agreement permits Dynamic Cables to use TS's encapsulated cores and to market TS-branded conductors in India, under a limited license that covers TS designs and intellectual property for manufacturing at Dynamic Cables’ facilities. The initial term is 12 months, with options to renew.

INDEX

urn:contentItem:6JJM-4XP3-S5GB-P4XF-00000-00Richardson Electronics to Present at the 16th Annual LD Micro Invitational on May 18, 20262026-05-12T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:37:39ZLexisNexisRichardson Electronics to Present at the 16th Annual LD Micro Invitational on May 18, 2026

AMERICAN DAILY STOCK REPORT

UNITED STATES EDITION

12 May 2026 00:26 EDT

ORIGINAL ANNOUNCEMENT [Text]

LAFOX, Ill., May 11, 2026 -- Richardson Electronics, Ltd. (NASDAQ: RELL), a global provider of engineered solutions for the alternative energy and power management markets, and custom displays for medical and industrial OEMs, today announced that it will be participating in the 16th Annual LD Micro Invitational at the Luxe Sunset Boulevard Hotel in Los Angeles, CA on May 18 19, 2026.

Richardson Electronics is scheduled to present on Monday, May 18, 2026, at 9:30 AM PT. Edward Richardson, Chairman and CEO and Wendy Diddell COO will be presenting on behalf of the Company.

To access the live presentation, please use the following information:

LD Micro Invitational XVIDate: Monday, May 18, 2026Time: 9:30 AM-9:55 AM PTWebcast: https://ldmicroevents.com/

If you would like more information about the event or to book 1x1 investor meetings with Richardson Electronics, please contact LD Micro at registration@ldmicro.com For more information on LD Micro, visit www.ldmicro.com.

About Richardson Electronics, Ltd.

Richardson Electronics, Ltd. is a leading global manufacturer of engineered solutions, green energy products, power grid and microwave tubes and related consumables; power conversion and RF and microwave components; high-value replacement parts, tubes, and service training for diagnostic imaging equipment; and customized display solutions.

More than 60% of our products are manufactured in LaFox, Illinois, Marlborough, Massachusetts, or Donaueschingen, Germany, or by one of our manufacturing partners throughout the world.

ORIGINAL ANNOUNCEMENT [Link]

Richardson Electronics to Present at the 16th Annual LD Micro Invitational on May 18, 2026 can be viewed via the link below.

value.Title: Shine on Florida : Streetleaf Expands Solar Streetlights in Greater Tampa Bay Area Pasco County’s new Pasadena Ridge development to install 300 new solar-powered streetlights to provide residents with enhanced safety and power reliability

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JJN-H933-SCMY-C02Y-00000-00Shine on Florida: Streetleaf Expands Solar Streetlights in Greater Tampa Bay Area2026-05-12T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:37:39ZLexisNexisPRODUCT / SERVICES ANNOUNCEMENTShine on Florida: Streetleaf Expands Solar Streetlights in Greater Tampa Bay AreaPasco County’s new Pasadena Ridge development to install 300 new solar-powered streetlights to provide residents with enhanced safety and power reliability

TAMPA, Fla., May 12, 2026 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) -- Streetleaf, the leading provider of solar-powered streetlight services in the United States, today announced its next installation in Pasco County, Florida. The company will now be lighting the streets of Pasadena Ridge, a new master-planned community in Dade City, Fl through an agreement with Homes by WestBay. The installation expands Pasco County’s position as one of the highest concentrations of solar streetlights in the U.S. with more than 5,400+ streetlights.

“We’re excited to continue building upon our Shine on Florida initiative with another installation in Pasco County,” said Liam Ryan, CEO of Streetleaf. “With less than a month away from the start of hurricane season, we’re increasing safety and resiliency for homeowners without them having to lift a finger. We look forward to continuing to scale our solar lighting infrastructure across the state to build more storm-resilient communities.”

Pasadena Ridge now hosts 129 of Streetleaf’s solar-powered streetlights throughout the development, allowing the community to operate independently from the power grid. With built-in battery backup, 24/7 monitoring and powered by 100% renewable energy, the lights stay on during outages and extreme weather events and add an extra layer of reliability and safety for growing communities. Following the first phase of the installation at Pasadena Ridge, Streetleaf will install another 173 solar-powered streetlights in the community over the next few months.

“Choosing to work with Streetleaf to provide our homeowners with a high-quality, off-grid solution goes hand-in-hand with our mission to deliver a better way of life for homeowners throughout the Tampa Bay region,” said Mark Metheny, President of Casa Fresca, a Homes by WestBay subsidiary. “Being able to enhance the safety, functionality and aesthetic of our communities at the onset with solar streetlights gives our homebuyers an instant advantage that we’re proud to provide.”

For more information about Streetleaf, please visit: www.Streetleaf.com.

ABOUT STREETLEAFHeadquartered in Tampa, Florida, Streetleaf specializes in integrating modern, renewable technology into communities through one of the most essential yet often-overlooked features—streetlights. Since its founding in 2019, Streetleaf has installed over 13,500 lights across the country, from Florida to California, and is rapidly growing to meet the rising demand for resilient, sustainable solutions. Streetleaf uses high-quality, dependable and proprietary hardware, software, and service to partner with land developers, builders, municipalities, utilities, and HOAs to create safer, greener communities. A carbon-neutral certified company, Streetleaf is also Darksky approved, and has offset more than 5.6 million pounds of CO2 and counting. Learn more at www.streetleaf.com.

MEDIA CONTACTFischTank PR streetleaf@fischtankpr.com

urn:contentItem:6JJD-VWS3-RWF8-6167-00000-00Phevs Provide The Sweet Spot Between Gasoline And Pure Electric Vehicles2026-05-11T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:37:39ZLexisNexisPhevs Provide The Sweet Spot Between Gasoline And Pure Electric Vehicles

Battery-powered vehicles with gasoline backup systems can act as a bridge between gas-powered cars and battery electric vehicles (BEVs). Referred to as plug-in hybrid electric vehicles (PHEVs), these hybrid solutions reduce range anxiety concerns among potential EV drivers while introducing consumers to electric propulsion technology in a familiar format.Pure EVs are also improving with each passing year, and a time is coming when models from brands like Massimo Group (NASDAQ: MAMO) will be as commonplace as...


Read More>>

value.Title: Findings from Damghan University Advance Knowledge in Renewable Energy (A fuzzy two-phase model for renewable energy system optimization under uncertainty: from operational to strategic scenario planning)
value.Overview: Ecology Daily News, ENERGY - RENEWABLE ENERGY, 363words

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JJG-P063-RW32-80GB-00000-00Findings from Damghan University Advance Knowledge in Renewable Energy (A fuzzy two-phase model for renewable energy system optimization under uncertainty: from operational to strategic scenario planning)2026-05-11T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:37:39ZLexisNexisENERGY - RENEWABLE ENERGYFindings from Damghan University Advance Knowledge in Renewable Energy (A fuzzy two-phase model for renewable energy system optimization under uncertainty: from operational to strategic scenario planning)


2026 MAY 11 (NewsRx) -- By a News Reporter-Staff News Editor at Ecology Daily News -- Researchers detail new data in renewable energy. According to news reporting out of Damghan, Iran, by NewsRx editors, research stated, "Integrating volatile renewable energy into power grids requires robust optimization methods that avoid restrictive probabilistic assumptions."


The news editors obtained a quote from the research from Damghan University: "This study proposes a novel two-phase fuzzy model bridging operational planning and strategic market analysis. Phase 1 uses a Mamdani-type fuzzy inference system to generate realistic scenarios for solar/wind availability and demand. Phase 2 formulates a fuzzy linear program to minimize costs under uncertainty, the results of which inform a fuzzy Nash equilibrium model to analyze market participant strategies. Validated on a 10-node model with real California ISO data, our approach reduces system costs by 18.7% and maintains 99.2% reliability, outperforming stochastic and robust benchmarks."
According to the news editors, the research concluded: "The model provides a practical decision-support tool for system operators and policymakers in renewable-dominated energy markets, demonstrating that fuzzy logic effectively captures real-world imprecision without complex data requirements."
For more information on this research see: A fuzzy two-phase model for renewable energy system optimization under uncertainty: from operational to strategic scenario planning. Energy Conversion and Management: X, 2026,30():101600. The publisher for Energy Conversion and Management: X is Elsevier.
A free version of this journal article is available at https://doi.org/10.1016/j.ecmx.2026.101600.
Our news journalists report that additional information may be obtained by contacting Hamed Fazlollahtabar, Department of Industrial Engineering, School of Engineering, Damghan University, Damghan, Iran.
Keywords for this news article include: Damghan University, Damghan, Iran, Asia, Energy, Oil & Gas, Renewable Energy.
Our reports deliver fact-based news of research and discoveries from around the world. Copyright 2026, NewsRx LLC

value.Title: Longroad Energy's Sun Pond Solar + Storage Project Achieves Commercial Operations

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JHC-0C53-RS65-K1HV-00000-00Longroad Energy's Sun Pond Solar + Storage Project Achieves Commercial Operations2026-05-06T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:36:54ZLexisNexisLongroad Energy's Sun Pond Solar + Storage Project Achieves Commercial Operations

Longroad Energy, a U.S.-based energy developer, owner and operator, announced today that Sun Pond, its 111 MWdc solar and 85 MWac / 340 MWh storage project, has achieved commercial operations. Ava Community Energy (Ava) and San Jos Clean Energy (SJCE) have contracted for the output of Sun Pond via long-term Power Purchase Agreements (PPAs). The project will generate enough electricity annually to power approximately 35,000 average American homes.

Ava Community Energy is a not-for-profit public power provider for more than 2 million residents and businesses in California's Alameda and San Joaquin Counties. San Jos Clean Energy is the not-for-profit electricity generation provider for homes and businesses in the City ofSan Jos, Santa Clara County, California.

"With Sun Pond now operational, Longroad is pleased to be expanding access to renewable energy for customers in the greater Bay Area," said Charles Spiliotis, Chief Investment Officer and co-founder of Longroad Energy. "Sun Pond's battery storage system adds firm, flexible capacity ensuring low-cost, clean power is available when the grid needs it most."

"The beginning of commercial operation for Sun Pond is an exciting milestone for Ava and our partnership with Longroad Energy," said Howard Chang, CEO of Ava Community Energy. "As the first of three planned projects we're advancing together, it brings us closer to our ambitious decarbonization goals. Just as importantly, it supports grid reliability and our ongoing commitment to deliver competitively priced renewable energy to our customers."

"Bringing Sun Pond online is another important step in delivering reliable, clean energy for our community," said Lori Mitchell, Director of San Jos Clean Energy. "Projects like this help ensure we meet growing energy needs while maintaining dependable service for our residents and businesses."

Sun Pond is projected to avoid over 145,000 metric tons of CO2 emissions annually, the equivalent of taking nearly 34,000 gasoline-powered passenger cars off the road each year.

Sun Pond is part of the Longroad Sun Streams Complex, a four-project complex totaling nearly 1.6 GW of solar and storage capacity located in Maricopa County, Arizona. The entire Longroad Sun Streams Complex is providing more than $300 million in benefits to Arizona schools and communities through its long-term leases with the Arizona State Land Department and tax remittances.

Longroad employed the Gridstack battery energy storage system from U.S.-based energy storage platform provider Fluence for the Sun Pond BESS. Sun Pond utilizes First Solar's photovoltaic modules, Nextpower's smart trackers, and Sungrow's solar inverters.

McCarthy Building Companies (McCarthy) was the engineering, procurement and construction (EPC) contractor. More than 300 people were employed across all contractors and teams at peak construction. NovaSource Power Services and Longroad's affiliate Longroad Energy Services will provide comprehensive operations and maintenance services.

value.Title: Amid US data centre boom, HMC makes $1bn retreat

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JHJ-2733-RYKX-40Y5-00000-00Amid US data centre boom, HMC makes $1bn retreat2026-05-07T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:37:39ZLexisNexisMARKETWATCHPg. 17Amid US data centre boom, HMC makes $1bn retreatBEN WILMOT

Investment banker turned fund manager David Di Pilla has dramatically shaken up his beaten-down HMC Capital empire, with its listed digital trust selling off a $1bn data centre in Chicago, warning fewer of the US complexes will be built than expected.

The sector has become a global sensation, with the predicted spending surge set to boost US economic growth. However, Mr Di Pilla said only two out of every 10 data centres proposed in the US would actually be built, and he was instead pouring capital back into Australia.

Mr Di Pilla predicted Australia would offer more sustainable energy supply, and pointed to bans on data centre projects in 14 US states. The company's DigiCo Infrastructure REIT also hit planning hurdles in Los Angeles and is planning on getting out of that market.

Mr Di Pilla is instead backing the expansion of his DigiCo trust's Sydney data centre, which is winning customers much faster than expected.

The market welcomed the shift, with HMC Capital shares jumping 17.1 per cent to $2.95 and DigiCo Infrastructure REIT up 25 per cent to $2.95 on Wednesday.

Mr Di Pilla said the pace of technological change was dramatically increasing, and he called out the power of the AI revolution in driving demand. But the pullback from much of the US -operation follows investors losing faith in the offshore expansion strategy and DigiCo's share price collapsing from its $5 float price.

The company indicated it would also look at selling out of the LA assets once it had gained the upside from those facilities.

After the deal, the data centre trust will effectively have $1bn in liquidity, with some of that also being paid back to investors. The sale is part of a bigger shift to simplify the sprawling HMC Capital empire, which has stumbled not only in data centres but also in healthcare real estate, where it is a Healthscope landlord, and private equity investing.

On that front, the company -effectively terminated the fund it used to drive the Chemist Warehouse-Sigma deal, after the trust copped heavy losses this year.

Mr Di Pilla cited its early successes but noted the clash between running a corporate activist strategy in the listed market and the demands of gatekeepers such as asset consultants that wanted it to invest widely. The fund has instead been stuck in slumping stocks such as Lendlease, Baby Bunting and Ingenia Communities. It sold out of most of its stake in Lifestyle Communities, leaving it with about $100m in cash.

Mr Di Pilla touted HMC Capital's ability to combine renewable energy, where it took a large bet after buying a $950m portfolio of wind, storage and solar projects from Brookfield, and data centres. That energy bet weighed on the company's balance sheet, but the fund manager insists it will pay off, despite not revealing the company's long-term plans for the -assets. Mr Di Pilla is trying to win back the faith of the sharemarket after his investors all but abandoned HMC Capital because its high growth ambitions of becoming a $50bn empire were not in keeping with tougher times. But he has pointed to cost cutting and the firm's ability to pivot in a world that he said was about to get tougher.

"The Middle East crisis is going on, a lot of uncertainty around the world, rising domestic rates here in Australia, the cost of capital is going up, liquidity is tightening," Mr Di Pilla said at the Macquarie Australia Conference. "It's a good time to be getting the balance sheet in strong shape," he added, saying opportunities may come up as it had $4bn in firepower.

Real estate remains the company's mainstay earner, and that division is now benefiting from the rerating of retail property, where it is heavily invested.

Mr Di Pilla also flagged further expansion in private credit, noting the differences in the local market, where investors are getting good risk-adjusted returns against widely reported difficulties in the US. HMC Capital stuck to guidance of at least 40c operating earnings per share.

However, the group's wings have been clipped by its share market slump. Little mention was made of the $50bn assets under management target that was once a hallmark; instead, the focus shifted to ongoing cost savings. HMC Capital is also engaging the hedge fund-dominated consortium that controls hospital operator Healthscope. Mr Di Pilla said the company's HealthCo fund and another offshore landlord had presented an alternative plan to the PurposeCo scheme put forward by Healthscope. But he said he remained willing to engage on the issue.

value.Title: Nuvve to Provide First Quarter Ended March 31, 2026, Financial Update Investor Conference Call to be Held Friday, May 15, 2026, at 5:00 PM Eastern Time (2:00 PM PT)

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JHM-TCT3-S1VY-11GK-00000-00Nuvve to Provide First Quarter Ended March 31, 2026, Financial Update2026-05-07T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:37:39ZLexisNexisNuvve to Provide First Quarter Ended March 31, 2026, Financial UpdateInvestor Conference Call to be Held Friday, May 15, 2026, at 5:00 PM Eastern Time (2:00 PM PT)SAN DIEGO

Nuvve Holding Corp. ("Nuvve") (Nasdaq:NVVE), a global leader in grid modernization and vehicle-to-grid (V2G) technology, will provide financial results for the first quarter ended March 31, 2025 after market close on Friday, May 15, 2026.

Conference Call Details
Nuvve will hold a conference call to review its financial results for the first quarter ended March 31, 2026, along with other company developments at 5:00 PM Eastern Time (2:00 PM PT), Friday, May 15, 2026.

To participate in the call, please dial (888) 349-0097 or (412) 902-4245; Passcode: 7689896, or register for and listen via a live webcast, which is available in the 'Events' section of Nuvve's investor relations website at https://investors.nuvve.com/ .

In addition, a replay of the call will be made available via Nuvve's investor relations website, or by calling (844) 512-2921 or (412) 317-6671, Access code 10204408 through June 15, 2026.

About Nuvve

Nuvve (Nasdaq: NVVE) is a global technology leader accelerating the electrification of transportation through its proprietary vehicle-to-grid (V2G) platform. Nuvve's mission is to lower the cost of electric vehicle ownership while supporting the integration of renewable energy sources, including solar and wind. For more information, please visit nuvve.com.

Nuvve Forward Looking Statements

This press release contains forward-looking statements or forward-looking information within the meaning of the U.S. Private Securities Litigation Reform Act of 1995. Forward-looking statements may be identified by the use of forward-looking terms such as "may," "will," "expects," "believes," "aims," "anticipates," "plans," "looking forward to," "estimates," "projects," "assumes," "guides," "targets," "forecasts," "continue," "seeks" or the negatives of such terms or other variations on such terms or comparable terminology, although not all forward-looking statements contain such identifying words. Forward-looking statements include, but are not limited to, statements concerning the Company's expectations, plans, intentions, strategies, prospects, business plans, product and service offerings, new product launches, potential clinical successes, and other statements that are not historical facts. Nuvve cautions you that these forward-looking statements are subject to numerous risks and uncertainties, most of which are difficult to predict and many of which are beyond the control of Nuvve. Such statements are based upon the current beliefs and expectations of management and are subject to significant risks and uncertainties that could cause actual outcomes and results to differ materially. Some of these risks and uncertainties can be found in Nuvve's most recent Annual Report on Form 10-K and subsequent periodic reports filed with the Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC). Copies of these filings are available online at www.sec.gov , https://investors.nuvve.com/ or on request from Nuvve. These factors should not be construed as exhaustive and should be read in conjunction with the other cautionary statements that are included in the Nuvve's filings with the SEC. Such forward-looking statements speak only as of the date made, and Nuvve disclaims any intention or obligation to update or revise any forward-looking statements, whether as a result of new information, future events or otherwise. Readers of this press release are cautioned not to place undue reliance on these forward-looking statements, since there can be no assurance that these forward-looking statements will prove to be accurate. This cautionary statement is applicable to all forward-looking statements contained in this press release.

value.Byline: Jami Ganz and Brian Niemietz New York Daily News
value.Title: Jane Fonda, CNN alum pay tribute
value.Overview: Daily News (New York), NEWS; Pg. A11, 759words

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JHK-PS13-S5KB-21WB-00000-00Jane Fonda, CNN alum pay tribute2026-05-07T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:37:39ZLexisNexisNEWSPg. A11Jane Fonda, CNN alum pay tributeJami Ganz and Brian Niemietz New York Daily News

Photo Caption: Turner Enterprises CEO Ted Turner addresses the Newsmaker Luncheon on renewable and alternative energy at the National Press Club April 19, 2011 in Washington, DC. Chip Somodevilla Getty Images Photo Caption: From left, television journalist Wolf Blitzer, television journalist Richard Roth, television journalist Ingrid Formanek and businessman Ted Turner attend the 13th Annual National CableACE Awards on January 12, 1992 at the Pantages Theatre in Hollywood, California. Getty Images

Ted Turner, the "singular" CNN founder, billionaire philanthropist and pioneer of the 24-hour cable news cycle, is being remembered for his contributions to journalism and the world at large, following his death. He was 87.

Turner, who also founded Turner Network Television (TNT) and Turner Classic Movies (TCM), died Wednesday while surrounded by his family near his Tallahassee, Fla. home, amid a battle with Lewy body dementia. Two-time Oscar winner and activist Jane Fonda - to whom Turner was married from 1991 to 2001 - celebrated his legacy with a lengthy Instagram post praising him for teaching her how to hunt, fish and think strategically.

"He swept into my life, a gloriously handsome, deeply romantic, swashbuckling pirate and I've never been the same," she wrote.

Fonda also praised Turner for taking care of her and allowing her to do the same.

"To be needed and cared for simultaneously is transformative," Fonda wrote. "Ted Turner helped me believe in myself. He gave me confidence."

She also praised his environmental activism. "I loved Ted with all my heart. I see him in heaven now with all the wildlife he helped bring back from extinction - the black footed ferrets, the prairie dogs, Big Horned sheep, Mexican Gray Wolf, the Yellowstone wolf pack, bison, the red cockaded woodpecker and so many more, they're all gathered at the pearly gates applauding and thanking him for saving their species."

Her post included a photo of the pair embracing.

CNN staples of past and present, including Wolf Blitzer, Christiane Amanpour, Katie Couric, CEO Mark Thompson and former CNN president Walter Isaacson, also paid tribute to Turner.

"'This is where the news comes first, and your vacations and everything else comes second,'" Blitzer recalled Turner saying upon hiring him in May 1990. "He was so determined to make sure that the news was strong and reliable and fair and he inspired me and so many other journalists to work and report the news fairly and accurately, and if possible, break those stories first."

Blitzer, who will "miss him a lot," remembered Turner as an "idealistic guy," who also emphasized to him the importance of CNN being seen nationally and internationally: "He felt that if people all over the world were seeing the same news, maybe the world would be a little bit more peaceful."

Amanpour remembered Turner as exemplifying "the amazing American success story, the businessman, the visionary, the revolutionary, who created really a media revolution." She said that any and everything in the 24/7 news cycle "was a copy" of Turner, "and not the best copies."

"He was the original and he made us all proud and he made us all hopeful. He made us all strive for his vision of a better world," continued Amanpour.

Couric, one of the self-described "CNN OGs," praised Turner as "a singular figure - in a world of colorful media moguls, he was technicolor."

"Ted was enormously proud of the network he created and the journalists who worked there. He was also a committed environmentalist and philanthropist who left the world much better than he found it," she continued. "Thank you, Ted, for the impact you had on the world and for doing it your way."

"There are no worthy successors to Ted Turner," Thompson said during a Wednesday afternoon appearance on CNN, while recalling his state of mind upon taking the head job.

Warner Bros. Discovery CEO David Zaslav reportedly reflected on Turner's legacy in a memo to staffers, highlighting brands like Turner Broadcasting System (TBS) - formerly WJRJ and WTCG - which he inherited in 1970, as well as the launches of TNT and TCM.

"Ted's influence is woven throughout Warner Bros. Discovery," wrote Zaslav. "The brands he built and championed remain central to who we are, and they continue to reflect his belief in creative risk, cultural impact, and global reach. ... Ted Turner changed our industry forever."

During the Investigative Journalism Summit in London, Isaacson remembered Turner as "the most fearless journalist I've ever seen."

"Ted's love of classic films inspired him to create a channel dedicated to preserving them. He considered TCM one of his greatest accomplishments," said TCM. "His passion continues to inspire us all as we work to fulfill his mission."

The Atlanta Braves, which Turner owned from 1976 to 1996, remembered him as a "good friend" and "one of a kind - a brilliant businessman, consummate showman and passionate fan of this beloved Braves." The team credited Turner for having "helped make us who we are today."

value.Title: April 28, 2026: Orrick Bolsters IP Bench with Senior USPTO Attorney Hire

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JHM-KD23-RXGW-R1KF-00000-00April 28, 2026: Orrick Bolsters IP Bench with Senior USPTO Attorney Hire2026-05-07T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:37:39ZLexisNexisANNOUNCEMENTSApril 28, 2026: Orrick Bolsters IP Bench with Senior USPTO Attorney Hire

Orrick, Herrington & Sutcliffe has bolstered its IP practice by recruiting a senior attorney from the U.S. Patent and Trademark Office (USPTO). The hire strengthens the firm's ability to handle complex patent issues as Orrick expands in technology and telecommunications sectors.

The new attorney will join on the 28th of April, 2026, bringing extensive experience from the USPTO in patent examination and litigation, aligning with Orrick's aim to deliver comprehensive legal solutions for clients in fast-moving industries.

Orrick's managing partner framed the hire as a signal of the firm's commitment to top-tier IP services and a deep understanding of patent law. The move fits within a broader strategy to adapt to the evolving landscape of intellectual property rights in the digital era.

INDEX

SECTION 1 ORRICK, HERRINGTON & SUTCLIFFE LLP PROFILE

SECTION 1 ORRICK, HERRINGTON & SUTCLIFFE LLP PROFILE

1.1 ACTIVITIES

Orrick, Herrington & Sutcliffe LLP, founded in 1863, is a global law firm headquartered in San Francisco, California. With over 1,100 lawyers across 25 offices, it serves technology, life sciences, and energy clients, specializing in M&A, IP, and venture capital. In 2024, it advised Coatue Management on $11.35 billion in investments, earning recognition as a top firm for startups and SaaS companies. Its culture fosters collaboration, diversity, and innovation, with key lawyers like Mark Solakian driving tech deals. Competing with Cooley, Orrick stands out for its tech-sector expertise and client-side advice. Recent initiatives include expanding AI and cybersecurity practices.

Mission

Orrick, Herrington & Sutcliffe LLP (Orrick) stands as a global leader in the legal industry, renowned for innovation, collaboration, and inclusivity. With over 25 worldwide offices and expertise spanning Technology & Innovation, Energy & Infrastructure, Finance, and Life Sciences & HealthTech, Orrick provides forward-thinking legal services tailored to client needs. Recognized for excellence in venture capital, renewable energy transactions, and high-stakes litigation, the firm prides itself on its commitment to diversity and cutting-edge solutions.

Vision

Orrick envisions establishing itself as a trusted global partner, offering innovative legal solutions that empower clients to grow, innovate, and succeed in rapidly evolving industries. Their goal is to redefine legal services by blending expertise with creativity to deliver comprehensive advice.

Values

Orrick's foundation is built upon the following core values:

Excellence: Upholding the highest standards of professionalism and quality in every service.

Teamwork: Encouraging collaboration within and beyond the firm to achieve outstanding outcomes.

Innovation: Committed to redefining legal practices through creative, client-centric approaches.

Perseverance: Tenaciously working to deliver the best possible outcomes for clients.

Inclusivity: Fostering a diverse and equitable environment where everyone is empowered to thrive.

By combining these principles, Orrick continues to elevate its reputation as a frontrunner in the global legal landscape.

1.2 SUMMARY

value.Title: Amid US data centre boom, HMC makes $1bn retreat

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JHH-WK33-RX99-J025-00000-00Amid US data centre boom, HMC makes $1bn retreat2026-05-07T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:37:39ZLexisNexisBUSINESSPg. 13Amid US data centre boom, HMC makes $1bn retreatBen Wilmot

Investment banker turned fund manager David Di Pilla has dramatically shaken up his beaten-down HMC Capital empire, with its listed digital trust selling off a $1bn data centre in Chicago, warning fewer of the US complexes will be built than expected.

The sector has become a global sensation, with the predicted spending surge set to boost US economic growth. However, Mr Di Pilla said only two out of every 10 data centres proposed in the US would actually be built, and he was instead pouring capital back into Australia.

Mr Di Pilla predicted Australia would offer more sustainable energy supply, and pointed to bans on data centre projects in 14 US states. The company's DigiCo Infrastructure REIT also hit planning hurdles in Los Angeles and is planning on getting out of that market.

Mr Di Pilla is instead backing the expansion of his DigiCo trust's Sydney data centre, which is winning customers much faster than expected.

The market welcomed the shift, with HMC Capital shares jumping 17.1 per cent to $2.95 and DigiCo Infrastructure REIT up 25 per cent to $2.95 on Wednesday.

Mr Di Pilla said the pace of technological change was dramatically increasing, and he called out the power of the AI revolution in driving demand. But the pullback from much of the US -operation follows investors losing faith in the offshore expansion strategy and DigiCo's share price collapsing from its $5 float price.

The company indicated it would also look at selling out of the LA assets once it had gained the upside from those facilities.

After the deal, the data centre trust will effectively have $1bn in liquidity, with some of that also being paid back to investors. The sale is part of a bigger shift to simplify the sprawling HMC Capital empire, which has stumbled not only in data centres but also in healthcare real estate, where it is a Healthscope landlord, and private equity investing.

On that front, the company -effectively terminated the fund it used to drive the Chemist Warehouse-Sigma deal, after the trust copped heavy losses this year.

Mr Di Pilla cited its early successes but noted the clash between running a corporate activist strategy in the listed market and the demands of gatekeepers such as asset consultants that wanted it to invest widely. The fund has instead been stuck in slumping stocks such as Lendlease, Baby Bunting and Ingenia Communities. It sold out of most of its stake in Lifestyle Communities, leaving it with about $100m in cash.

Mr Di Pilla touted HMC Capital's ability to combine renewable energy, where it took a large bet after buying a $950m portfolio of wind, storage and solar projects from Brookfield, and data centres. That energy bet weighed on the company's balance sheet, but the fund manager insists it will pay off, despite not revealing the company's long-term plans for the -assets. Mr Di Pilla is trying to win back the faith of the sharemarket after his investors all but abandoned HMC Capital because its high growth ambitions of becoming a $50bn empire were not in keeping with tougher times. But he has pointed to cost cutting and the firm's ability to pivot in a world that he said was about to get tougher.

"The Middle East crisis is going on, a lot of uncertainty around the world, rising domestic rates here in Australia, the cost of capital is going up, liquidity is tightening," Mr Di Pilla said at the Macquarie Australia Conference. "It's a good time to be getting the balance sheet in strong shape," he added, saying opportunities may come up as it had $4bn in firepower.

Real estate remains the company's mainstay earner, and that division is now benefiting from the rerating of retail property, where it is heavily invested.

Mr Di Pilla also flagged further expansion in private credit, noting the differences in the local market, where investors are getting good risk-adjusted returns against widely reported difficulties in the US. HMC Capital stuck to guidance of at least 40c operating earnings per share.

However, the group's wings have been clipped by its share market slump. Little mention was made of the $50bn assets under management target that was once a hallmark; instead, the focus shifted to ongoing cost savings. HMC Capital is also engaging the hedge fund-dominated consortium that controls hospital operator Healthscope. Mr Di Pilla said the company's HealthCo fund and another offshore landlord had presented an alternative plan to the PurposeCo scheme put forward by Healthscope. But he said he remained willing to engage on the issue.

urn:contentItem:6JHC-T6K3-RYKS-N3TG-00000-00Amid US data centre boom, HMC makes $1bn retreat2026-05-06T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:37:39ZLexisNexisAmid US data centre boom, HMC makes $1bn retreat

Document links may not lead to an active page. Page maintenance is at the discretion of the publisher.

Ben Wilmot

Investment banker turned fund manager David Di Pilla has dramatically shaken up his beaten-down HMC Capital empire, with its listed digital trust selling off a $1bn data centre in Chicago, warning fewer of the Untied States complexes will be built than expected.

The sector has become a global sensation, with the predicted spending surge set to boost US economic growth. Mr Di Pilla said, however, that only two out of every ten data centres proposed in the United States would actually be built and he is instead pouring capital back into Australia.

Mr Di Pilla predicted Australia would offer more sustainable energy supply and pointed to bans on data centre projects in 14 US states. The company's DigiCo Infrastructure REIT also hit planning hurdles in Los Angeles and is planning on getting out of that market.

Mr Di Pilla is instead backing the expansion of his DigiCo trust's Sydney data centre, which is winning customers much faster than expected.

The market welcomed the shift, with HMC Capital shares jumping 13.9 per cent to $2.88 and DigiCo Infrastructure REIT up 14.8 per cent to $2.71 in early morning trade on Wednesday.

Mr Di Pilla said the pace of technological change was dramatically increasing and called out the power of the AI revolution in driving demand. But, the pullback from much of the US operation follows investors losing faith in the offshore expansion strategy and DigiCo's share price collapsing from its $5 float price.

The company flagged it would also look at selling out of the LA assets once it had gained the upside from those facilities. After the deal, the data centre trust will effectively have $1bn in liquidity, with some of that also being paid back to investors.

The sale is part of a bigger shift to simplify the sprawling HMC Capital empire which has stumbled not only in data centres but also in healthcare real estate, where it is a Healthscope landlord, and private equity investing.

On that front, the company effectively terminated the fund it used to drive the Chemist Warehouse-Sigma deal, after the trust copped heavy losses this year. Mr Di Pilla cited its early successes but noted the clash between running a corporate activist strategy in the listed market and the demands of gatekeepers like asset consultants which wanted it to invest widely.

The fund has instead been stuck in slumping stocks like Lendlease, Baby Bunting and Ingenia Communities. It sold out of most of its stake in Lifestyle Communities, leaving it with about$100m in cash.

Mr Di Pilla touted HMC Capital's ability to combine renewable energy, where it took a large bet after buying a $950m portfolio of wind, storage and solar projects from Brookfield, and data centres. That energy bet weighed on the company's balance sheet, but the fund manager insists it will pay off, despite not revealing the company's long-term plans for the assets.

Mr Di Pilla is trying to win back the faith of the sharemarket after his investors all but abandoned HMC Capital because its high growth ambitions of becoming a $50bn empire were not in keeping with tougher times. But, he has pointed to cost cutting and the firm's ability to pivot in a world that he said was about to get tougher.

"The Middle East crisis is going on, a lot of uncertainty around the world, rising domestic rates here in Australia, the cost of capital is going up, liquidity is tightening," Mr Di Pilla said at the Macquarie Australia Conference. "It's a good time to be getting the balance sheet in strong shape," he said, flagging opportunities may come up as it has $4bn in firepower.

Real estate remains the company's mainstay earner, and that division is now benefiting from the rerating of retail property, where it is heavily invested.

Mr Di Pilla also flagged further expansion in private credit, noting the differences in the local market, where investors are getting good risk-adjusted returns against widely-reported difficulties in the US.

He is urging investors to stick with the stock, saying it will stick to its knitting by finding opportunities in undervalued sectors that align with the "megatrends" it wants to capitalise on, and then creating investment products.

He was bullish enough about conditions for HMC Capital to stick to guidance of at least 40c operating earnings per share.

The group's wings have been clipped, however, by its share market slump. Little mention was made of the $50bn assets under management target that was once a hallmark of the company; instead, the focus shifted to ongoing cost savings.

HMC Capital is also engaging the hedge fund-dominated consortium that controls hospital operator Healthscope. Mr Di Pilla said the company's HealthCo fund and another offshore landlord had presented an alternative plan to the PurposeCo scheme put forward by Healthscope. But, Mr Di Pilla says he remains willing to engage on the issue and suggested the group was comfortable with potential rent reductions which have been flagged to the market.

value.Title: R.I. public media workers say they are unionizing
value.Overview: The Boston Globe, BUSINESS; Pg. D,3,999, 1104words

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JH5-6G93-SC56-B12M-00000-00R.I. public media workers say they are unionizing2026-05-05T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:37:39ZLexisNexisBUSINESSPg. D,3,999R.I. public media workers say they are unionizingABSTRACT

Talking Points business briefs

BODY

MEDIA

R.I. public media workers unionizing

Employees at Ocean State Media, Rhode Island's joint NPR and PBS organization, announced Monday that they are forming a union with SAG-AFTRA. A majority of the organization's hosts, reporters, and producers that would be represented by the unit signed a petition to unionize, which was delivered to management Monday, according to a SAG-AFTRA press release. "We are proud to create public media every day for, and with, the people of Rhode Island and Southeastern Massachusetts," the Ocean State Media organizing committee said in a statement. "We believe that in order to sustainably and effectively serve that mission, the voices of staff need to be represented in our organization's decision-making." The unionization effort comes roughly two years after Rhode IslandPBS and The Public's Radio merged to form Ocean State Media. The organization named Pam Johnston, who previously served as the general manager of GBH News, as its first chief executive. — AIDAN RYAN

ENERGY

Data centers are driving carbon prices upward in Northeast

Data centers are driving a surge in carbon prices in the Northeast, helping push CO2 costs in the region past California and raising the prospect of higher energy costs for consumers. The price to emit a so-called short ton of CO2 into the atmosphere under the Regional Greenhouse Gas Initiative — a market covering 10 states, including all of New England — jumped 31 percent last week to $47.56. Traders are betting that Virginia's planned return to the market in July will boost demand for permits because the state is a major hub for data centers. The rally has sent prices past California's record CO2 price of $44 set in 2024. The US doesn't have a federal-level carbon price and instead has two state-run markets in California and Washington, the latter of which has a price around $66. That's in addition to the RGGI, which has existed since 2009 and is the country's oldest program. Carbon markets put a price on each ton of CO2 emitted into the atmosphere, typically by industry. The goal is to steadily reduce emissions while incentivizing the adoption of renewable energy and other clean technologies. RGGI covers only CO2 emissions from the power sector, which are expected to increase with the growing artificial intelligence-led demand for data centers. RGGI also risks adding to consumer bills, which are already rising because of the boom. — BLOOMBERG NEWS

ARTIFICIAL INTELLIGENCE

Trump looks to vet new AI models

President Trump, who promoted a hands-off approach to artificial intelligence and gave Silicon Valley free rein to roll out the technology, is considering the introduction of government oversight over new AI models, according to US officials and people briefed on the deliberations. The administration is discussing an executive order to create an AI working group that would bring together tech executives and government officials to examine potential oversight procedures, according to US officials, who declined to be identified in order to discuss deliberations over sensitive policies. Among the potential plans is a formal government review process for new AI models. In meetings last week, White House officials told executives from Anthropic, Google, and OpenAI about some of those plans, people briefed on the conversations said. The working group is likely to consider a number of oversight approaches, officials said. But a review process could be similar to one being developed in Britain, which has assigned several government bodies to ensure that AI models meet certain safety standards, people in the tech industry and the administration said. — NEW YORK TIMES

FINANCIAL SERVICES

Anthropic, investment firms team up on AI integration

Anthropic is teaming up with several large investment firms to create a venture that will help companies integrate artificial intelligence tools into their systems, the latest example of the deepening ties between Wall Street and the AI industry. Private equity firms Blackstone and Hellman & Friedman and investment bank Goldman Sachs through its investment funds are among the financial backers in the new firm, which will work with companies to deploy Anthropic's AI model Claude. In announcing the creation of the firm on Monday, Anthropic and the investment firms said the technology around AI was changing so rapidly that many companies were finding it challenging to integrate Claude. The backers of the new firm said it would work with Anthropic's engineers to help companies deploy Claude, which has abilities that "change on a monthly or even weekly basis." — NEW YORK TIMES

FINANCE

N.Y. Fed president says rates will come down 'at some point'

Federal Reserve Bank of New York president John Williams said interest rates will need to come down "at some point" if inflation returns to the US central bank's 2 percent target, as he expects. "We will at some point need to be lowering interest rates to reflect the fact that inflation is lower," Williams told reporters Monday after a speech in New York City. "Inflation is higher this year than previously expected, so that pushes off a date of lowering interest rates, in my view, but it doesn't change that basic story." The Fed left its benchmark interest rate unchanged last week, though three officials dissented against the "easing bias" in the central bank's postmeeting statement, favoring language signaling the next rate move could be either a hike or a cut. Williams told reporters he was "very comfortable" with the current language in the statement because he doesn't "see anything in the day-to-day" suggesting there's an argument for a rate hike in the near term. — BLOOMBERG NEWS

TRADE

Canada to spend $1.1B to offset Trump's tariffs on metal

The government of Canadian Prime Minister Mark Carney (right) unveiled measures worth $1.1 billion to help Canadian firms hurt by the Trump administration's changes to US tariffs on imports containing steel, aluminum, and copper. The change in early April put a 25 percent surcharge on the total value of products that contain these metals. Previously, the US applied a 50 percent tariff only to the actual metal content inside the products, but importers complained that regime was too complicated in practice. The change has been devastating to some Canadian businesses. Last month BRP Inc., which makes recreational vehicles such as Sea-Doo watercraft and Ski-Doo snowmobiles, withdrew its financial outlook for the 2027 fiscal year due to the tariff shift, which it expected would add $367 million in costs. —BLOOMBERG NEWs

value.Title: Southern Company Chairman, President and CEO Chris Womack Regarding the Passing of Ted Turner

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JHF-20B3-RWRK-G1G2-00000-00Southern Company Chairman, President and CEO Chris Womack Regarding the Passing of Ted Turner2026-05-06T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:37:40ZLexisNexisSouthern Company Chairman, President and CEO Chris Womack Regarding the Passing of Ted Turner

SOUTHERN CO ("SO-N") - Southern Company Chairman, President and CEO Chris - Womack Regarding the Passing of Ted Turner Southern Company Chairman, President and CEO Chris Womack said: "Ted Turner was a visionary who called Atlanta homean entrepreneur whose bold ideas helped transform our city into a global media hub and whose legacy resonates far beyond our region. Many people knew Ted as a pioneer in media and broadcasting, but he was also a passionate advocate for environmental stewardship, working alongside Southern Company to help pioneer the development of utility-scale solar energy. Through our partnership with Turner Enterprises and Turner Renewable Energy, we advanced several projects including Cimarron Solar, our first solar facility constructed in 2010 and built on Ted's New Mexico property, as well as Campo Verde Solar, and Morelos del Sol Solar in California.

These projects helped Southern Company establish its first utility-scale solar operations, expand our development expertise, and accelerate the integration of solar into our generation portfolio. Ted's pioneering spirit and unwavering commitment to making a difference changed the world for the better, inspiring countless individualsincluding myselfto pursue innovation with purpose and passion. On behalf of Southern Company, I extend my deepest condolences to his family, friends and all who were touched by his remarkable life and leadership. Atlanta, and indeed the world, is better for it." About Southern Company Southern Company (NYSE:SO) is a leading energy provider serving 9 million customers across the Southeast and beyond through its family of companies. The company has electric operating companies in three states, natural gas distribution companies in four states, a competitive generation company, a leading distributed energy solutions provider with national capabilities, a fiber optics network and telecommunications services. Our uncompromising values ensure we put the needs of those we serve at the center of everything we do and are the key to our sustained success, driven by nearly 30,000 employees dedicated to delivering exceptional service. To learn more, visitwww.southerncompany.com.

value.Title: KBRA Assigns Ratings to Sunrun Prometheus Issuer 2026-1, LLC

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JHC-9RJ3-S8JG-N0GS-00000-00KBRA Assigns Ratings to Sunrun Prometheus Issuer 2026-1, LLC2026-05-05T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:37:39ZLexisNexisKBRA Assigns Ratings to Sunrun Prometheus Issuer 2026-1, LLC

Key Highlights:

* KBRA assigns ratings to three classes of notes issued by Sunrun Prometheus Issuer 2026-1, LLC.

* Transaction collateralized by 38,706 residential solar leases and power purchase agreements (PPAs).

* Total Aggregate Discounted Solar Asset Balance (ADSAB) is approximately $844.4 million.

Original Press Release:

New York, May 5 -- Kroll Bond Rating Agency, LLC issued the following news release:

KBRA assigns ratings to three classes of notes issued by Sunrun Prometheus Issuer 2026-1, LLC. The transaction is collateralized by a diversified pool of 38,706 leases and power purchase agreements (PPAs) associated with residential solar photovoltaic installations (PV Systems). The total Aggregate Discounted Solar Asset Balance (ADSAB) based on a discount rate of 7.5%, consisting of the discounted payments of the leases and PPAs is approximately $844.4 million.

The three largest geographic concentrations include California, Puerto Rico, and Massachusetts, which together represent approximately 58.2% of the number of Systems and approximately 69.1% of the ADSAB. The portfolio consists of approximately 79.5% PPA agreements, 20.7% lease agreements, and -0.3% fully prepaid agreements by ADSAB of customer contracts with monthly payments. The weighted average original and remaining tenor of the PPAs and leases is 287 months and 261 months, respectively. The weighted average FICO of the underlying customers of the Systems is 744.

About KBRA

Kroll Bond Rating Agency, LLC (KBRA), one of the major credit rating agencies (CRA), is a full-service CRA registered with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission as an NRSRO. Kroll Bond Rating Agency Europe Limited is registered as a CRA with the European Securities and Markets Authority. Kroll Bond Rating Agency UK Limited is registered as a CRA with the UK Financial Conduct Authority. In addition, KBRA is designated as a Designated Rating Organization (DRO) by the Ontario Securities Commission for issuers of asset-backed securities to file a short form prospectus or shelf prospectus. KBRA is also recognized as a Qualified Rating Agency by Taiwan's Financial Supervisory Commission and is recognized by the National Association of Insurance Commissioners as a Credit Rating Provider (CRP) in the U.S.

Source: Kroll Bond Rating Agency, LLC

[Category: Renewable Energy, ESG and Sustainability]
